intro: The Civilian Complaint Review Board traces its origins to 1953, when the NYPD created it as an internal committee of three deputy police commissioners with no civilian members; a mid-1960s effort by Mayor John Lindsay to add civilians was reversed by a citywide ballot measure pushed by the police union. In 1993, Mayor David Dinkins and the City Council remade the CCRB into its current all-civilian form, independent of the police department, with subpoena power and the authority to recommend discipline in substantiated police-misconduct cases.
